introSection: Tatilya Express was a steel roller coaster previously located at Erbil Family Fun in Erbil, Erbil, Iraq. The name of the coaster is currently unknown, and Coasterpedia thus resorts to the last known name.

sections.title: History
sections.content: The coaster started its life at Tatilya in Istanbul, Istanbul, Turkey. This was an indoor park, making the ride an indoor coaster. It operated here from 1996 until the park's closure in April of 2004, after which it was relocated to Iraq. It would operate at Erbil Family fun until 2013, after which it would be taken down to make way for an expansion of the neighbouring mall.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: The train is designed to resemble a Engine.

fullText:
Tatilya Express was a steel roller coaster previously located at Erbil Family Fun in Erbil, Erbil, Iraq. The name of the coaster is currently unknown, and Coasterpedia thus resorts to the last known name.

The coaster started its life at Tatilya in Istanbul, Istanbul, Turkey. This was an indoor park, making the ride an indoor coaster. It operated here from 1996 until the park's closure in April of 2004, after which it was relocated to Iraq. It would operate at Erbil Family fun until 2013, after which it would be taken down to make way for an expansion of the neighbouring mall.



Yellow track and green supports.

The train is designed to resemble a Engine.
